title: No function to remove liquidity - liquidity permanently locked
reason: Incorrect. `openTrading()` sends LP tokens to `owner()`, not to the contract, so liquidity is not locked; the real issue is the opposite rug-pull risk captured in F-002.

title: No timelock on critical owner actions - instant rug pull possible
reason: Generic governance-centralization criticism, not a distinct exploitable bug beyond the specific blacklist and LP-control findings already reported.

title: Tax collected goes to single EOA wallet with no multisig
reason: Single-key trust and treasury-governance concerns are not by themselves protocol vulnerabilities here.

title: Uniswap V2 router hardcoded - no upgrade path
reason: Lack of upgradeability is a design choice, not a concrete exploit path for this target.

title: Owner can remove all transaction limits enabling large transfers
reason: This is a privileged tokenomics control, but by itself it does not create a concrete protocol-level exploit or loss scenario distinct from the other owner-abuse findings.

title: Infinite approval to Uniswap router on LP token
reason: `IERC20(uniswapV2Pair).approve(...)` is granted from the token contract, but LP tokens from `openTrading()` are minted to `owner()`, so the contract does not receive LP tokens to be drained through that approval.

title: Trading can only be opened once - no recovery if stuck
reason: `openTrading()` is atomic; if any step fails, the whole transaction reverts, so there is no partial stuck state from a failed call.

title: Bot check bypassable - returns false for contracts in first 3 blocks
reason: Ineffective anti-bot logic is not a reportable vulnerability without a concrete exploit causing protocol-level harm.

title: Swap threshold and amounts are fixed constants
reason: Parameter rigidity and price-impact tuning are design concerns, not concrete security vulnerabilities.

title: Public isBot() function exposes blacklist
reason: Public visibility of blacklist status does not create meaningful protocol harm.

title: SafeMath library is obsolete - Solidity 0.8+ has built-in overflow checks
reason: Informational code-style observation only; not a vulnerability.

title: Hardcoded router plus unlimited approvals can hand contract-held tokens to arbitrary code off-mainnet
reason: Too deployment-dependent and speculative for this target. Without evidence the contract is intended for or deployed on a chain where `0x7a250...` is untrusted, this is not a concrete reportable issue.
